markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games' repeated delays of GTA VI to its current November 19, 2026 release date have extended the window for major cultural and tech milestones, shaping trader focus on high-profile events like new music drops, AI model launches, and geopolitical shifts. Recent earnings calls and official statements emphasize additional polish as the reason for the latest six-month pushback, keeping the game on track amid industry scrutiny of development timelines. With roughly five months remaining, momentum centers on chart trajectories for anticipated albums, streaming benchmarks, and any confirmed announcements that could resolve specific outcomes before the launch window closes. Traders monitor precursor signals such as release strategies and public statements for clues on what will cross the finish line first.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The May 15 mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s third New York retrial on the Jessica Mann rape charge has reinforced trader expectations for limited additional incarceration. With the jury deadlocked and prosecutors weighing a fourth trial, the unresolved count joins an earlier mixed verdict still awaiting sentencing, while Weinstein’s separate 16-year California conviction remains under appeal. His six-plus years already served at Rikers, advanced age, documented health issues, and successful prior appeals create strong momentum toward time-served outcomes. These factors have driven the market-implied odds heavily toward No Prison Time, reflecting consensus that further convictions or upheld sentences appear unlikely in the near term.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2026 Stanley Cup Final pits the Carolina Hurricanes against the Vegas Golden Knights in a best-of-seven series opening June 2 at Lenovo Center in Raleigh. Carolina earned the higher seed with a dominant 53-22-7 regular season and 113 points, securing home-ice advantage after eliminating the Montreal Canadiens in the Eastern Conference Final. Vegas advanced by sweeping the Colorado Avalanche in the West and has posted strong recent form, including a 19-4-1 stretch under coach John Tortorella. Trader consensus reflects Carolina's edge in regular-season metrics and venue while acknowledging Vegas's playoff momentum, experience from its 2023 title, and depth contributions from players like Mitch Marner. The closely contested implied probabilities align with the matchup's competitive balance heading into Game 1.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping’s centralized control of the Chinese Communist Party and military, reinforced by repeated elite reshuffles and purges that have sidelined potential rivals, underpins trader expectations that he will remain in office through 2026. Official actions in recent months—including his active direction of the 15th Five-Year Plan, a May 2026 summit with U.S. leadership, and public messaging on national priorities—show no signs of diminished authority or institutional pressure for an early exit. Analysts widely anticipate Xi securing a fourth term at the 21st Party Congress in fall 2027, with succession planning deferred well beyond that point. While unforeseen health issues or sudden realignments remain low-probability risks, current conditions reflect entrenched dominance and the absence of credible challenges ahead of the next leadership transition cycle.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination market with the highest share in a fragmented field more than two years before voting begins, reflecting his national profile as California governor, term-limited status that frees him to campaign early, and ongoing visibility through public positions against the current administration alongside initial donor outreach. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez draws support from progressive voters while Kamala Harris benefits from name recognition tied to her recent general-election experience. Other governors such as Josh Shapiro, Pete Buttigieg, and Andy Beshear appeal to moderates seeking different ideological positioning. Early national polls show similar name-recognition patterns without consolidation, and surveys indicate many Democratic voters favor a more centrist nominee. No candidates have formally entered the race, leaving room for shifts ahead of the 2026 midterms and subsequent announcements.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination market with the highest share in a fragmented field more than two years before voting begins, reflecting his national profile as California governor, term-limited status that frees him to campaign early, and ongoing visibility through public positions against the current administration alongside initial donor outreach. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez draws support from progressive voters while Kamala Harris benefits from name recognition tied to her recent general-election experience. Other governors such as Josh Shapiro, Pete Buttigieg, and Andy Beshear appeal to moderates seeking different ideological positioning. Early national polls show similar name-recognition patterns without consolidation, and surveys indicate many Democratic voters favor a more centrist nominee. No candidates have formally entered the race, leaving room for shifts ahead of the 2026 midterms and subsequent announcements.
